findings: Species identity is correct: the illustrated bird matches a Yellow-rumped Warbler, specifically a Myrtle-like white-throated form, with gray streaked plumage, dark facial mask, yellow side patch and rump, white wing bars, long narrow tail, and dark passerine bill.

The plate’s common name, Setophaga coronata, Parulidae, exact length (4.7–5.5 in / 12–14 cm), wingspan (7.5–9.1 in / 19–23 cm), and weight (0.4–0.5 oz / 12–13 g) match the All About Birds identification page.

Habitat and behavior statements are supported by the live sources. The field-mark text is also supported, including yellow rump and side patches, white tail markings, large-headed full-bodied shape, long narrow tail, and white- versus yellow-throated forms.

Against the supplied photos, the plate correctly shows the small pointed dark bill, large dark eye, two yellow flank/rump regions, layered flight feathers with white wing bars, long tail, and anisodactyl feet. It depicts one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail; the far wing is naturally occluded in the perched profile.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears in Image 1. No material species, anatomy, or text errors found.

verification_sources.title: Yellow-rumped Warbler Identification — All About Birds
